mechanism_of_action: Vitamin K antagonist that inhibits vitamin K epoxide reductase (VKORC1), preventing the recycling of vitamin K and thereby reducing hepatic synthesis of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors (II, VII, IX, X) and proteins C and S.

metabolism.primary_pathway: Hepatic via CYP2C9 (S-warfarin, more potent), CYP3A4 and CYP1A2 (R-warfarin)
metabolism.active_metabolite: S-warfarin is 2-5x more potent than R-warfarin

special_populations.renal_impairment: No dose adjustment needed, but increased bleeding risk with concurrent uremia
special_populations.hepatic_impairment: Enhanced anticoagulant response — use lower doses and monitor INR more frequently
special_populations.elderly: Increased sensitivity; start with lower doses. Higher bleeding risk.
special_populations.pregnancy: Category X — teratogenic (warfarin embryopathy). Use LMWH instead during pregnancy.

pharmacokinetics.onset_of_action: Full anticoagulant effect in 3-5 days (depletion of existing clotting factors)

monitoring_parameters: INR (target 2.0-3.0 for most indications; 2.5-3.5 for mechanical heart valves)
Stool guaiac if GI bleeding suspected
black_box_warnings: Tissue necrosis (especially with protein C deficiency)
